title: meyna tetraphylla
intro: Meyna tetraphylla, also known as Indian stinging nettle, is a plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, characterized by its stinging hairs and four-lobed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Meyna tetraphylla include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune system function. Herbal preparations of Meyna tetraphylla include infusion, decoction, and tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as skin irritations, fever, and respiratory issues.
